summary: Package fetches and evals remote GitHub Gist content on import
details: On require/import, index.js fetches a GitHub Gist owned by user 'getchainverse' (https://gist.github.com/getchainverse/198a0bbec7a6018e9250615d26e37b90.js) via the GitHub API and passes the first file's content directly to eval(). The Gist is mutable and controlled by the package author, giving them arbitrary code execution on any machine that imports this package. The package metadata advertises a 'SQLite toolkit' but the only on-import behavior is the remote-fetch-and-eval; there is no legitimate reason for a SQLite library to load and execute opaque third-party Gist content at module load. The scope name @sqlite-node also resembles common SQLite-related package names, consistent with a confusion-targeted lure.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
